quoteFirst: For all the months the diurnal amount of cloud is greatest
quoteTime: about one o'clock in the afternoon

quoteLast: we noticed that the Lusitania was steering a zigzag course. Land had been in sight for three hours, distinctly visible twelve miles away. Looking through my glasses, I could see on the port side of the Lusitania, between us and land, what appeared to be a black, oblong object, with four dome-like projections. It was moving along parallel to us, more than two miles away. At times it slowed down and disappeared. But always it reappeared. All this time the Lusitania was zigzagging along. Later the Lusitania kept a more even course, and we generally agreed then that it was a friendly submarine we were watching. We had seen no other vessels except one or two fishing boats.
title:
Horrors and Atrocities of the Great War
Including the Tragic Destruction of the Lusitania
author: Marshall, Logan, 1884-1937; Gibbs, Philip, 1877-1962 [Contributor]; Parker, Gilbert, 1862-1932 [Contributor]; Thompson, Vance, 1863-1925 [Contributor]
